feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /b/ Random: If your gf offered you to crush your balls, would you do it?
entry.content: If your gf offered you to crush your balls, would you do it?

entry.title: /b/ Random: Thoughts so far?
entry.content: A new playground is being built in my area, it's for student & teacher dating, right? I hope it was because that's immediately what I saw In a pointless project in a pointless school, we were all shown a short film about a class that were told to all make simple pictures of dots on pieces of paper I later see that along with supposedly being compulsory, people's version of the dot all get in way with other's version of the dots, & make the world much more horribler So, what now? How do we perman

entry.title: /g/ Technology: The more AI model advance the more I see you can get better results than the mos…
entry.content: The more AI model advance the more I see you can get better results than the most advanced models with an open source cheap one and then editing the output by hand to fix mistakes. I literally get no errors shit with multiple characters using CAT and SD1.5 and gemini by simply fixing the mistakes by hand.

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Netflix, Paramount and Sony have had early talks about buying Letterboxd
entry.content: https://variety.com/2026/digital/ne ws/letterboxd-sales-talks-netflix-s ony-paramount-1236806379/ >Letterboxd, founded in 2011, has been shopping itself to interested parties in recent months. The app and website (which also recently launched an online video-rental service) is majority-owned by Canadian holding company Tiny, which acquired a 60% stake in Letterboxd in 2023 at a valuation of $50 million-$60 million. Letterboxd co-founders Matthew Buchanan and Karl von Randow own the remaining 40%

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: ITT: we think of ways NEET anons can un-mess up their lives
entry.content: NEETdom often results in one cutting themselves off from society, as well as progressing in any employment path. When NEETs are cut off from support (whether from the government or their family) the only end result they can see is usually suicide. Let's do something good, and brainstorm how these lost souls can make something of their lives!

Patch will rise again LATEST EVENTS: Ruby VS Maka DEATH BATTLE out now on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rgR zzGsEtCk RWBY V10 has been greenlit: https://x.com/OfficialRWBY/status/1 941582029952311673 Previous Thread: >>83

entry.content: Hang it up, unc.

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: How did this become Britain's normal / default style?
entry.content: This is the epitome of what the default lad in Britain looks like. I've lived in Coventry my whole life, but heard this style is everywhere. From teens to middle age men in their 60's, even. Tracksuits, gold chains, trainers, baseball caps. Then you have the slang that always goes with it like peng, swear down, mush, wagwan, creps, etc. The music tastes are DnB and UKG. It's the T-shirt and shorts of Britain for over 20 odd years. How did it evolve? Where did the slang come from?

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Can you complete the tasks in the DeepSWE benchmarks? If you can't, then it's a…
entry.content: Can you complete the tasks in the DeepSWE benchmarks? If you can't, then it's alright if you're automated away, yeah? What's the point of keeping you on if a clanker can do the same thing for $20? Maybe you can. Can the average software engineer complete them? Look at this: https://deepswe.datacurve.ai/data/v 1/tasks/prometheus-typed-label-sort ing That's the entire prompt, no internet access. Could you complete this? Can the average software engineer? GPT 5.6 Sol completed this in 34 minu

entry.title: /g/ Technology: > Copilot: Sorry, it looks like I’m not yet available in your region. If you’d l…
entry.content: > Copilot: Sorry, it looks like I’m not yet available in your region. If you’d like, you can turn off the Copilot New tab page by going to your browser settings. > ChatGPT: Unable to load site. Please try again later. If you are using a VPN, try turning it off. > Grok: This service is not available in your region. > Unfortunately, Claude is only available in certain regions right now. Please contact support if you think you’re getting this message in error. Feels good to be trad easter EVROPEAN.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Steam is having another record year for revenue
entry.content: https://alineaanalytics.substack.co m/p/steam-is-having-another-record- year >In the first half of 2026, we estimate that games on Steam generated $11.1B in gross revenue, the platform’s highest-ever half-year. That’s up 14.5% on H1 2025, and up 8% even on the holiday-heavy H2 2025, which is the more remarkable comparison given the back half of the year usually wins on seasonal sales and holiday buys. >Zoom out over the last decade and things get really crazy. There’s obviously a visible dip as

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: FF9 is weird
entry.content: Does anyone ever get the feeling that FF9 is kind of an odd one out in the series? FF7, 8 and 10 all have the same developers in charge, they feel pretty cohesive as a trilogy both in terms of story, tone and mechanics. 9 has a bunch of newcomers and spin-off merchants, and mechanically it's pretty different from the prior entries, it almost feels like it should have been a spin-off itself rather than a mainline title.

entry.title: /b/ Random: You are sucked into a time portal The last 4 numbers of your post in reverse is …
entry.content: You are sucked into a time portal The last 4 numbers of your post in reverse is the year you end up in. However if you get dubs the full numbers of your post are used instead and the year is BC

entry.title: /b/ Random: CREATE A CRYPTID/CREEPYPASTA!
entry.content: Work together in a thread and come up with a unique paranormal monster. Once you've all agreed on what it looks like and what it does, spread the rumor in as many places online as possible that it's real and that you saw it. Come up with a believable story. Photoshop some pictures, create audio recordings, or whatever else you'd like. Most importantly, have fun.

entry.content: what have i "learned" from being subjected to this stupid shitty life of absolute dog shit misery for so many years...let's see, hmm...how about nothing? yeah, nothing. people don't "learn" anything here, they just suffer. you can't even say living in this world is "enjoyable" because it mostly isn't. humans need to perish, i mean holy shit, it is wayyyyy overdue. my parents suck, my old "friends" suck, even my ex-girlfriend sucks. i fucking hate whatever this life is supposed to be. i want to g

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Holy Fuck. Waterfox Is By Far The Best Browser Now
entry.content: >Always based on latest Firefox ESR >All of the good shit from Firefox >None of the obnoxious telemetry or redesign shit from Mozilla >No useless AI shovelware "features" >No VPN promo shit, "experiments", or "recommended" extensions >Built-in preferences for those who want an even more toned down UI >Built-in ad-blocking on par with Brave's engine (which is 99.9% compatible with uBlock Origin), but completely optional for those want straight up uBO >Can still use encrypted media like widevine i

entry.title: /g/ Technology: >be me >bored af on a friday night, brain rotting from switching between 17 diff…
entry.content: >be me >bored af on a friday night, brain rotting from switching between 17 different package managers >pip install -r requirements.txt >npm install --save >cargo fetch >rustup update >sudo apt install whateverthefuck >decide enough is enough >time to make something for the boys >open vscode like a true schizo >start writing Crystal Dependency Manager (crys) >one short, memorable command to rule them all >no more memorizing autistic flags for every language (pip install crys) >now instead of tha

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: Why do normie artists hate AI art?
entry.content: I'm saving up for a custom PC with an rtx 5070 and 32 GB of ddr5 ram, and I want to generate my own anime series with 90s cel shading animation with straight shotacon stuff in it, and I get shit because I'm using AI instead of wanting to use humans which is expensive and labor intensive.

entry.title: /o/ Auto: I've never bought a car before but i checked cars.com for my area. This is what …

entry.title: /g/ Technology: I took a few programming classes throughout my life, but never stuck with it. Ho…
entry.content: I took a few programming classes throughout my life, but never stuck with it. How do you go from all the boring highly theoretical stuff about algorithms and math problems to actually making apps and games? I've heard coders say time and time again that they learn on the job and that their degrees helped more with the mindset rather than the concrete details.

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: >no penis
entry.content: >no penis

entry.title: /g/ Technology: I want to understand the AI hype. How does it justify the trillions of dollars i…
entry.content: I want to understand the AI hype. How does it justify the trillions of dollars in spend? Is it about lowering costs for programming? I'm a nocoder, so I can't judge it on that. When I see people use "AI" it's always just as a Google search replacement. That and maybe summarizing text. Those features are useful, but is it enough to drive the industry, economically speaking? Will enough people watch ads or pay for subscriptions to finance all that spend?

entry.content: hello gng i am john bart simpson i have made the BEST most AWESOME pc checker known to man!!!!!!! (WINDOWS 13 ONLY!!!!) @echo off title bart color 0E mode con: cols=60 lines=20 powershell -NoProfile -Command "Add-Type -AssemblyName PresentationFramework; [System.Windows.MessageBox]::Show(' hi there xro')" >nul 2>&1 timeout /t 2 /nobreak >nul powershell -NoProfile -Command "Add-Type -AssemblyName PresentationFramework; [System.Windows.MessageBox]::Show(' my name is bart simpson')" >nul 2>&1 timeo

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: How do we balance Cavaliers in Fire Emblem?
entry.content: Cavaliers have always been better than the foot locked units in FE with some exceptions In some games the difference is not that bad like 6 or 7 where the cavaliers are still clearly better because they just have better movement and can use rescue to fary you around the map but other units like Dieck or Rutger who can still contribute enough in a playthrough but thent there game like path of Radiance or 3 Houses where Cavaliers and Fliers just dominate with barely any Infantry coming close Their
